title: Racosperma varium
intro: Racosperma varium, also known as Acacia varia, is a species of thorny shrub or small tree native to Australia. The health benefits of Acacia varia include its ability to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, wounds, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Acacia varia include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Acacia varia is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and promote wound healing.
taxonomy: Racosperma varium is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It is native to Australia and was previously known as Acacia varia. The plant is often referred to as the variable acacia or prickly acacia. Racosperma varium has been reclassified from Acacia varia within the legume family. It is a shrub or small tree with a wide distribution in Australia's eastern regions.
